'''Golm''', also known as '''Bothawui Prime''', was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Both system]]. A purple [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]], it was the fourth planet in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] and was orbited by 23 [[Moon/Legends|moons]], as well as an [[Golm asteroid belt|asteroid belt]].<ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

==History==
Like it's "twin", [[Ganash]], the planet was named after a hero from [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] mythology, [[Golm Fervse'dra]].<ref name="guide" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> a [[Battle of Bothawui/Legends|battle]] was fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in orbit of the planet. A Republic [[Republic Navy/Legends|naval]] task force regrouped in Bothawui Prime's orbit after several vicious confrontations with a Separatist fleet led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. They were the only line of defense between him and [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] itself. As Grievous's [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] swooped in to attack the Republic [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], groups of [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TE]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] hidden on the asteroids of Bothawui Prime's rings, opened fire on the vessels' unguarded sterns and turned the tide of battle, helping to stop his assault on Bothawui.<ref name="DOAD">{{TCW|Downfall of a Droid}}</ref>
{{Planet-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The gas giant identified as "Bothawui" in ''[[The Clone Wars: Season One]]'' was [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' as Golm, one of the two gas giants of the Both system and given an alternate name, Bothawui Prime.<ref name="Atlas">{{JCFCite|url=posts/30367601|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2009-08-21|time=7:17 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150125011537/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-95}}</ref>
